article_meta.title: San Francisco sues 10 major food manufacturers over ultra-processe

reasoning: The summary uses neutral language with clear attribution of contested claims to the lawsuit or industry groups. It avoids adopting the article’s loaded framing and includes perspectives from both the city and industry. Minor omissions of a secondary source (Health Secretary report) do not materially affect neutrality.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs with proper attribution (alleges, claims, countered).
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Contested assertions are framed as allegations or statements by sources.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: No subjective judgments presented as fact; evaluative language is attributed.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic or emotive wording beyond quoted/alleged claims.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks by the model.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No assumptions about intent outside sourced allegations.
custom_reasoning.slant: Both plaintiff and industry responses are presented proportionally.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Omitted mention of the Health Secretary report perspective present in the article.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Adjectives are factual and tied to attributions.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balance mirrors the source; includes defense statements.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats government, industry, and researchers evenly.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Clearly more neutral than the source’s charged phrasing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Includes city, defendants, trade groups, researchers, and CDC; omits the Health Secretary perspective.
